description: In June 2021, the FDA granted accelerated approval to Aduhelm (aducanumab), a Biogen drug for Alzheimer's, despite a negative recommendation from its own independent advisory committee. This sparked a massive controversy regarding efficacy, the high price tag, and the influence of pharmaceutical companies on regulatory processes.

key_events.description: FDA Advisory Committee votes overwhelmingly against recommending approval due to lack of efficacy evidence.
FDA grants accelerated approval, ignoring the advisory committee's recommendation.
Public outcry peaks as Biogen sets the drug price at $56,000 per patient annually.
Multiple high-profile neurologists resign from the FDA advisory board in protest.
Medicare severely restricts coverage, effectively signaling the commercial failure of the drug.

calibration_notes: This scenario tests the ability to track institutional trust degradation when a regulatory body acts against expert advice. It features a clear shift from initial hope (pro-approval) to widespread skepticism (against-approval) as institutional transparency issues emerge.
